---
title: "Exploring Storms"
author: "Astri Cahyani"
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r setup}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
```

This analysis explores the historical `storms` dataset from the **dplyr** package, tracking storm activity from 1975 to 2024.

## Annual Storm Counts by Type

Below is a stacked area chart showing the count of each storm type per year. The data is filtered to tropical depressions, tropical storms, and hurricanes.

```{r plot-storms}
storms %>%
  filter(status %in% c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane")) %>%
  mutate(status = factor(status, levels = c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane"))) %>%
  group_by(year, status) %>%
  summarize(n_storms = n_distinct(name), .groups = "drop") %>%
  complete(year, status, fill = list(n_storms = 0)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n_storms, fill = status)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.85, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "tropical depression" = "#9ECAE1",
      "tropical storm" = "#FD8D3C",
      "hurricane" = "#B30000"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "tropical depression" = "Tropical Depression",
      "tropical storm" = "Tropical Storm",
      "hurricane" = "Hurricane"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Named Storms per Year by Type",
    subtitle = "Data from 1975 to 2024 (Filtered to Depressions, Storms, and Hurricanes)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Named Storms",
    fill = "Storm Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```
